question.text: Under SEBI IA Regulations, which type of investment adviser must mandatorily appoint a compliance officer?

answer.text: All investment advisers regardless of structure
Only non-individual investment advisers (body corporate, LLP, partnership firm)
Only individual IAs with more than 150 clients
Only IAs registered with BSE Administration & Supervision Ltd

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Under SEBI IA Regulations 2013, the mandatory appointment of a compliance officer applies only to non-individual investment advisers (body corporate, LLP, partnership firm). Individual IAs are not required to appoint a separate compliance officer — the individual IA is personally responsible for compliance.
**The Trap:** Candidates may assume all intermediaries need a compliance officer. While this is true for many SEBI-regulated entities, individual IAs are specifically exempted due to the nature of their practice structure.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Compliance officer requirements are tested across NISM Series X-A and Series XII (Portfolio Managers). For portfolio managers, compliance officer appointment is mandatory regardless of structure.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** If you're a non-individual IA, ensure your compliance officer has adequate knowledge of SEBI IA Regulations and is empowered to flag issues. A compliance officer in name only invites regulatory risk.
